events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need assess field condition: control wiring in same raceway as power for VFDs & motor controls. Need compare against NEC 430 and mfr manuals. Need determine pass/fail. Control wiring in same raceway as power is generally not allowed? NEC 430? Motor control wiring? Let's reason.

Scenario: heavy industrial, VFDs & motor controls, tested/inspected. Observed: control wiring in same raceway as power. Reference NEC 430, mfr manuals. Need decide if fail. Control wiring (e.g., low-voltage control, communication) in same raceway/conduit as power conductors can cause induction, noise, EMI, overheating? NEC requirements: Class 2/3 control circuits may be in same raceway with power if insulation equal? Actually NEC 725.136 says Class 2 and Class 3 circuits shall not be in same raceway with conductors of electric light, power, Class 1 circuits unless barrier. But motor control circuit conductors (Class 1) can be in same raceway with motor power conductors? NEC 430.72(B) maybe control circuit conductors in same enclosure/cable with motor power conductors if insulation rating sufficient. Let's recall.

NEC 430 Part VI Control Circuits. 430.71 Scope. 430.72 Control Circuit Supply. 430.73 Overcurrent Protection. 430.74 Disconnecting Means. 430.75 Control transformer. 430.76? Actually 430.72(B) maybe control circuit conductors in same raceway as motor circuit conductors if insulation rating not less than motor circuit conductors. Let's check memory: NEC 430.72(B) "Conductors of Control Circuits. Control circuit conductors shall be considered protected by the motor branch-circuit short-circuit and ground-fault protective device where they are rated not less than the motor branch-circuit conductors and are installed in the same raceway." Hmm.

Actually NEC 430.72(B): "Conductors of control circuits shall be considered protected by the motor branch-circuit short-circuit and ground-fault protective device(s) where they are rated not less than the motor branch-circuit conductors and are installed in the same raceway." That implies same raceway allowed if rated equal. But VFD control wiring? VFD manufacturer manuals typically require control/communication wiring to be separated from power wiring, run in separate conduit, shielded, crossing at 90 degrees, to avoid EMI. So field condition control wiring in same raceway as power likely violates manufacturer manual and NEC separation for Class 2/3 low-voltage control circuits. If control wiring is low-voltage (e.g., 24V, 120V control) in same raceway as 480V power, it's a fail due to EMI, induction, possible insulation, code. Need decide fail, risk medium/high. Component condition installed-defective and non-compliant. Need include references: NEC 430.72(B)? Actually if control circuit conductors are in same raceway but not rated equal, fail. Also manufacturer manual separation requirements. NEC 300.3(C) maybe conductors of same circuit only? Actually 300.3(C) Conductors of Different Systems. Conductors of circuits rated 1000 volts or less, including power-limited fire alarm, remote-control, signaling, and communications circuits, shall be permitted to occupy the same enclosure if each conductor has insulation rating equal to max voltage applied to any conductor within enclosure or barrier. But control wiring in same raceway as power may be allowed if insulation rating adequate. However VFD control wiring is often shielded low voltage and manufacturer requires separation. The prompt says reference material on hand: NEC 430, mfr manuals. The observed field condition is control wiring in same raceway as power. Need compare. Since manufacturer manuals typically require separation, and NEC 430 may require control circuit conductors in same raceway to have insulation not less than power conductors. If observed control wiring likely lower insulation (e.g., 300V) in same raceway with 600V power, fail. Also NEC 725.136 for Class 2/3 separation. But scope is VFDs & motor controls; cont…
